Engineering Management Institute

Simple Guidelines for Guest Bloggers

Welcome, and thank you for contributing to the EMI blog! 

We’ve kept this process as straightforward as possible. We know your time is valuable.

Step #1: Pick Your Dates

  • Visit our blog post-scheduling sheet and look for yellow rows; those are open spots.
  • Simply reply to the email thread with the date(s) you’d like to claim. Please limit yourself to one post per month. You can also email us directly at content@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org.
  • The themes listed in Column D are suggestions only; feel free to write on any topic relevant to engineering leadership, project management, or professional development.
  • Once you’ve selected a date, add it to your calendar and plan to submit your draft two weeks before your publication date.

Step #2: Write Your Post

What makes a great EMI post:
  • 600–800 words in length
  • Informal in tone — personal stories and real experiences resonate best with our audience
  • Structured around key points (e.g., “5 Steps to Building a High-Performing Team”)
  • Focused on one clear takeaway
  • Solution-oriented rather than problem-focused
  • Relevant to engineers and AEC professionals looking to grow personally and professionally
  • Conversational — a good test: read your draft aloud. If it sounds stiff, rewrite it the way you’d say it
  • Includes a short author bio at the end with a link back to your website

A note on formatting:

Our readers scan before they read. Help them find the good stuff quickly by using bullet points, numbered lists, bold text, and short paragraphs.

Multimedia:

You’re welcome to include photos or videos, but it’s not required. We’ll select an image for your post if you don’t provide one.

Sample posts for reference:

A longer post  |  A shorter post

Originality and republishing:

Please do not submit content identical to previously published posts. Minor modifications to existing posts are acceptable. You are welcome to republish your post on other platforms after it goes live on the EMI blog.

Step #3: Submit Your Draft

  • Email your draft to content@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org. Please include a short author bio and a headshot we can use with your post.
  • If significant edits are needed, we’ll send the revised draft back to you for review before publication.
  • Your post will go live on your selected publication date. We’ll follow up with sharing details at that time.

A note on links and references:

Please include all external links and resources in your draft submission. We are not able to add, remove, or modify links after an article has been published.

Ownership

  • You retain full ownership of your content.
  • You may republish it on other websites.
  • Engineering Management Institute reserves the right to republish content on affiliated websites.


Thanks for contributing!

Anthony Fasano, PE
Engineering Management Institute
Author of Engineer Your Own Success
